Module com.example.escriturarapida
Package com.example.escriturarapida.model

Class Time

java.lang.Object
com.example.escriturarapida.model.Time
public class Time extends Object
Model that manages all the timers in the game "EscrituraRapida"

Controls three independents:

  • The countdown to the current level
  • The total count of seconds played in the game
  • The alert flashing effect when time is critical (<=5 seconds)

See Also:
  • GameController

  • Constructor Details

    • Time

      public Time()
      Default constructor. Creates an instance of Time with 20 initial seconds and counters at zero.

  • Method Details

    • getSeconds

      public int getSeconds()
      Returns the remaining seconds of the current level.
      Returns:
      Remaining seconds as an integer.

    • start

      public void start(Runnable onTick, Runnable onTimeOut)
      Starts the countdown of the current level.

      Decrements seconds each second. On each tick executes onTick to update the view. When it reaches 0, stops the timeline and executes onTimeOut.

      Parameters:
      onTick - Runnable executed each second (e.g.: update the label).
      onTimeOut - Runnable executed when time reaches 0 (e.g.: validate input).

    • resetSecond

      public void resetSecond(int seconds)
      Resets the level seconds to the indicated value. Used before starting each new level.
      Parameters:
      seconds - Amount of seconds to assign for the new level.

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the level countdown if it is running.

    • totalSeconds

      public void totalSeconds()
      Starts the total count of seconds for the match.

      Increments totalSeconds each second from the start of the match until stopTotalSeconds() is called.

    • stopTotalSeconds

      public void stopTotalSeconds()
      Stops the total match count if it is running.

    • getTotalSeconds

      public int getTotalSeconds()
      Returns the total seconds elapsed during the match.
      Returns:
      Total seconds played as an integer.

    • switchColor

      public void switchColor(Runnable onRed, Runnable onWhite)
      Starts the timer flicker animation when time is critical.

      Activates only when seconds is less than or equal to 5. Alternates between onRed and onWhite every 300ms using the flag flickerActive, generating a visual effect of urgency.

      Parameters:
      onRed - Runnable executed on "active" frames (e.g.: set text to red).
      onWhite - Runnable executed on "inactive" frames (e.g.: set text to white).

    • stopSwitchColor

      public void stopSwitchColor()
      Stops the timer flicker animation if it is running.
